notes.content: Preference will be given to junior-level applicants with the highest grades overall who have satisfactorily completed the following required course work. Required for admission : Students must have a cumulative UC transferable GPA of 3.0 (3.4 for TAG) One year of single variable calculus with a minimum grade of B in each course Recommended for admission : Additional course work in multivariable calculus, linear algebra, and differential equations is strongly recommended Students may specialize in Mathematical Biology, or concentrate in Data Science or Mathematical Finance. Additional information is available at http://ps.uci.edu/undergraduates . Students pursuing the Data Science or Mathematical Finance Concentration should consider Physics 7C, Stats 7, and Math 10. Those interested in the Mathematical Biology Specialization should consider Bio Sci 93, Bio Sci 94, and Math 10. Important : Articulation of General Chemistry is based on the completion of the entire year. Students wanting information on course-to-course equivalencies (semester to quarter) should contact the Physical Sciences Student Affairs Office at: pssazot@uci.edu. NOTE : In fulfillment of the requirements below, a single course may be used only once. For information regarding the AP and IB examination credit policies refer to the UCI General Catalogue. For more information regarding the UC Irvine Transfer Admission Guarantee Program, please visit TAG
notes.contentHtml:
Preference will be given to junior-level applicants with the highest grades overall who have satisfactorily completed the following required course work.


Required for admission:

Students must have a cumulative UC transferable GPA of 3.0 (3.4 for TAG)


  • One year of single variable calculus with a minimum grade of B in each course


Recommended for admission:


  • Additional course work in multivariable calculus, linear algebra, and differential equations is strongly recommended


Students may specialize in Mathematical Biology, or concentrate in Data Science or Mathematical Finance. Additional information is available at http://ps.uci.edu/undergraduates.

Students pursuing the Data Science or Mathematical Finance Concentration should consider Physics 7C, Stats 7, and Math 10. Those interested in the Mathematical Biology Specialization should consider Bio Sci 93, Bio Sci 94, and Math 10.


Important: Articulation of General Chemistry is based on the completion of the entire year. Students wanting information on course-to-course equivalencies (semester to quarter) should contact the Physical Sciences Student Affairs Office at: pssazot@uci.edu.



NOTE: In fulfillment of the requirements below, a single course may be used only once.


For information regarding the AP and IB examination credit policies refer to the UCI General Catalogue. 


For more information regarding the UC Irvine Transfer Admission Guarantee Program, please visit TAG
